lede: A look at April 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Kofi Annan and Jacques Brel, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Windows XP
events.desc: Windows XP reaches its standard End Of Life and is no longer supported.

events.title: Barack Obama
events.desc: U.S. President Barack Obama and Russian President Dmitry Medvedev sign the New START Treaty.

events.title: BOAC Flight 712
events.desc: BOAC Flight 712 catches fire shortly after takeoff. As a result of her actions in the accident, Barbara Jane Harrison is awarded a posthumous George Cross, the only GC awarded to a woman in peacetime.

events.title: Netherlands
events.desc: The Netherlands and West Germany sign an agreement to negotiate the return of German land annexed by the Dutch in return for 280 million German marks as Wiedergutmachung.

events.title: Grace Hopper
events.desc: A team of computer manufacturers, users, and university people led by Grace Hopper meets to discuss the creation of a new programming language that would be called COBOL.

events.title: North American T-6 Texan
events.desc: A Royal Canadian Air Force Canadair Harvard collides with a Trans-Canada Airlines Canadair North Star over Moose Jaw, Saskatchewan, killing 37 people.

events.title: Western Australia
events.desc: The Australian state of Western Australia votes to secede from the federation in a non-binding referendum, although efforts to implement the result prove unsuccessful.

events.title: Venus de Milo
events.desc: The Venus de Milo is discovered on the Aegean island of Milos.

events.title: Alexander I of Russia
events.desc: Czar Alexander I, the Russian Emperor and the Grand Duke of Finland, officially announces the transfer of the status of the Finnish capital from Turku to Helsinki.

events.title: Manuel I Komnenos
events.desc: Manuel I Komnenos succeeds his father John II Komnenos as Byzantine Emperor.

births.name: Kofi Annan
births.line: Ghanaian economist and diplomat, 7th Secretary-General of the United Nations

births.name: Melvin Calvin
births.line: American chemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Margaret Thatcher, English politician, first female Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

observances.text: Buddha's Birthday, also known as Hana Matsuri, "Flower Festival" (Japan)
Christian feast day: Pope Dionysius of Alexandria
Christian feast day: Julie Billiart of Namur
